info.name: Wejha Student Platform API
info.description:
Complete collection of every API endpoint used specifically by the Wejha **student** mobile/web app. Excludes organizer/admin-only endpoints (QR attendance scanning, generic email sender) which belong to a separate staff-facing tool.

All routes live in `routes/web.php` under `/api/*`. Auth is a custom Bearer token (not Sanctum) issued at register/login/google-login/reset-password and validated by `ApiTokenMiddleware`.

**Auth flow**: call Register, Login, or Google Login first. The response's `student.api_token` is auto-saved to the `api_token` environment variable by a test script on each request. All protected requests use `Authorization: Bearer {{api_token}}`.

**Response envelope**: every endpoint returns `{ "success": boolean, "message": "...", ...data }`.

**Suggested end-to-end run order**: Register → Login → Get Profile → Update Profile → List Events → Get Event → Get Event Evaluations → Register for Event → List My Registrations → Get My Ticket → Get Survey Questions → Submit Survey Answers → Cancel Registration → List Notifications → Mark Notification Read → Change Password → Logout.

item.name: Auth

request.description:
Create a new student account.

Validation:
- name: required|string|max:255
- email: required|email|max:255|unique:users
- password: required|string|min:6
- phone_number, gender(male|female), academic_year, school_name, specialization(scientific|literary|علمي|أدبي): all nullable

Response 201: `{ success, message, student: { id, name, email, api_token } }`. Auto-assigns the `Student` role and sets `status=active`.

exec: pm.test('status is 201 or 422 (email may already exist)', () => pm.expect([201, 422]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description:
Log in with email + password.

Validation: email required|email, password required|string.

Responses: 200 success `{ success, message, student: { id, name, email, api_token } }`; 401 invalid credentials; 403 account inactive.

Sends a WelcomeEmail on the user's very first login.

Log in / auto-register via Firebase Google Sign-In.

Validation: id_token required|string (a real Firebase ID token from the mobile app's Google Sign-In flow — cannot be generated from Postman).

Response 200: `{ success, message, student: { id, name, email, api_token, avatar } }`. 400 if email couldn't be extracted; 401 if the Firebase token is invalid/expired.

Request a password reset code. Validation: email required|email|exists:users,email.

Generates a random 6-digit code, stores it hashed in `password_reset_tokens` (valid 60 minutes), and emails it to the user via the branded Wejha email template.

Response 200: `{ success: true, message: "...code sent to your email." }`. Check the inbox and set the `reset_code` environment variable manually to test Reset Password below.

Complete the password reset using the code emailed by Forgot Password.

Validation: email required|email|exists:users,email, code required|string, password required|min:6|confirmed (needs matching `password_confirmation`).

On success, the password is updated, the reset code is invalidated, and a fresh `api_token` is issued (old token is invalidated as a security measure).

Response 200: `{ success, message, student: { id, name, email, api_token } }`. Response 400 if the code is wrong or older than 60 minutes.

Invalidate the current session by nulling the stored `api_token` server-side. There was previously no way to log out at all — the token lived forever. After this call the token used in the request is permanently dead; the next Login/Register call will issue a new one.

Response 200: `{ success: true, message: "...Logged out successfully." }`.

item.description: Public account creation/login endpoints plus password recovery and logout. Test scripts auto-save `api_token` to the environment.

item.name: Profile

request.description:
Get the authenticated student's full profile. Previously, profile data was only ever returned once at register/login (name, email only) — this exposes everything: name, username, email, phone_number, gender, academic_year, school_name, specialization, avatar, preferred_language, preferred_theme, notification_preferences, status, last_login_at.

Response 200: `{ success, profile: {...} }`.

Partially update the authenticated student's profile. All fields are optional (`sometimes` validation) — send only what changed.

Fields: name, phone_number, gender(male|female), academic_year, school_name, specialization(scientific|literary|علمي|أدبي), avatar (URL string), preferred_language(ar|en), preferred_theme(light|dark|system), notification_preferences (object/array).

Note: email and password are NOT editable here — email has no update endpoint (would require re-verification, out of scope), password has its own dedicated endpoint below. Also no file-upload endpoint exists for avatar; it only accepts a URL string.

Also works with PUT. Response 200: `{ success, message, profile: {...} }`.

Change password while logged in (different from the Forgot/Reset Password flow, which is for when the student can't log in at all).

Validation: current_password required|string, new_password required|min:6|confirmed (needs matching `new_password_confirmation`).

Response 200: `{ success: true, message }`. Response 401 if current_password doesn't match.

item.description: Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{api_token}}`. View/edit the student's own profile and credentials — entirely new; previously no way to read back or update profile data at all.

item.name: Events

request.description:
List all published events — upcoming/ongoing ones first (soonest first), then ended events after (most recently ended first). Ended events are NOT hidden — they stay visible so students can still see past events and watch the recording if one was added.

Response 200: `{ success, events: [{ id, title_ar, title_en, description_ar, description_en, type(seminar|workshop|exhibition), speaker, event_date, event_time, end_date, end_time, venue, capacity, banner_image_url, cover_image_url, has_ended, can_register, recording_url }] }`.

`end_date`/`end_time` are null for single-day events with no set duration. `has_ended` is computed from the event's actual end (end_date/end_time if set, otherwise event_date/event_time). `can_register` is `!has_ended` — use it to disable the registration button client-side (the backend also rejects registration attempts for ended events). `recording_url` is null until the event has actually ended, even if an admin added it early.

Get a single event's details by UUID.

Response 200: `{ success, event: {...same shape as list item...} }`. Response 404 if not found.

NEW — discover which pre/post-event surveys exist for an event, and get the `evaluation_id` needed by the Surveys endpoints below. Previously there was NO way for the app to find this ID at all, making survey submission unreachable in practice.

Admins now link surveys to an event directly from the Event create/edit form in Filament (two Select fields: "Pre-Survey" / "Post-Survey", each filtered to survey_templates of the matching type) — no separate relation-manager tab anymore. Under the hood this still writes to the same `event_evaluations` table, so this endpoint's behavior/response shape is unchanged.

Only returns active evaluations (`is_active=true`). An event with no survey selected for a slot simply omits it from the array (never errors).

Response 200: `{ success, evaluations: [{ id, evaluation_type(pre|post), is_active, template: { id, name_ar, name_en, status, category } }] }`. Response 404 if the event doesn't exist.

item.description: Public event browsing — no authentication required.

item.name: Bookings

request.description:
Register the authenticated student for an event.

Validation: event_id required|uuid|exists:events,id; source string|in:web,mobile,admin (optional, defaults "web").

Business rules: 400 event not found / event has already ended / registration not open yet / registration closed; 422 event fully booked; 422 already registered.

On success (201) status is `pending` if the event requires approval, else `approved`; a `qr_hash` ticket is generated and an EventRegistrationEmail is sent.

Response 201: `{ success, message, registration: { id, status, qr_hash } }`

NEW — "my bookings": list the authenticated student's own event registrations, newest first. Previously there was no way to list a student's own bookings at all.

Optional query param `status`: `upcoming` (future, non-cancelled events) or `past` (past events or cancelled registrations). Omit to get everything.

Response 200: `{ success, registrations: [{ id, status, qr_hash, source, registered_at, checked_in_at, event: { id, title_ar, title_en, type, event_date, event_time, venue, banner_image_url } }] }`

NEW — view a single registration's ticket, including the `qr_hash` needed to render the entry QR code. Previously `qr_hash` was returned ONLY once, in the response of Register for Event — if the student closed the app, the ticket was unrecoverable.

Scoped to the authenticated user (returns 404, not another user's data, if the registration belongs to someone else).

Response 200: `{ success, registration: { id, status, qr_hash, source, registered_at, checked_in_at, event: {...} } }`. Response 404 if not found or not owned by the caller.

Cancel one of the authenticated student's own registrations. As of this build the endpoint is scoped to the caller (previously any logged-in student could cancel ANY registration by guessing its ID — fixed alongside this collection).

Sets status to `cancelled`.

Response 200: `{ success: true, message: "تم إلغاء التسجيل بنجاح." }`. Response 404 if not found/not owned.

item.description: Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{api_token}}`. Register/cancel event bookings and view them afterward.

item.name: Surveys

request.description:
Fetch the actual question list (configured via the Filament admin dashboard's Google-Forms-style survey builder) for a pre/post-event evaluation, so the app can render the form BEFORE submitting.

`{{evaluation_id}}` comes from Get Event Evaluations above.

`options` (only present for `multiple_choice`/`checkbox` questions) is a plain array of option label strings in admin-defined display order, e.g. `["ممتاز", "جيد", "مقبول"]` — render each as a radio (multiple_choice) or checkbox (checkbox) list in that order. There is no "other/free-text" option support yet.

Note: per-question `description`/`help_text`/`score` fields were removed from the system (no longer exist on the question at all, admin UI no longer offers them) — the question object only has the fields listed below now.

Also returns `already_submitted` (true if this user has already answered this evaluation — use to avoid re-showing the form) and `can_submit` (false + a `reason` if the user isn't registered for the event, or if it's a post-survey and they haven't checked in yet).

Response 200: `{ success, evaluation: { id, event_id, evaluation_type }, already_submitted, can_submit, reason, questions: [{ id, type, question_text_ar, question_text_en, options, is_required, sort_order }] }`.

Response 404 if evaluation not found; 403 if the survey/template is closed/inactive.

Submit answers to a pre/post-event evaluation. `{{evaluation_id}}` is the `event_evaluations.id` (from Get Event Evaluations), NOT the event id or survey_template id.

Validation: answers required|array|min:1; answers.*.question_id required|uuid|exists:survey_questions,id; answers.*.response_text nullable|string (text/rating/number/date/email/phone questions); answers.*.response_json nullable|array (multiple_choice/checkbox questions).

For `multiple_choice`/`checkbox` questions, send `response_json` as an array containing the exact selected option string(s) from that question's `options` list (from Get Survey Questions above) — e.g. `["ممتاز"]` for a single choice, or `["خيار أ", "خيار ج"]` for a checkbox question with multiple selections. The second answer in this example body is a placeholder — set `question_id_choice` to a real multiple_choice/checkbox question's id, or remove it if not testing that case.

Business rules: 400 evaluation not found / user not registered / required question missing / question doesn't belong to this template; 403 evaluation closed; a "post" evaluation additionally requires the user to have checked in first.

Each question can only be answered ONCE per evaluation (DB unique constraint) — resubmitting throws a generic error.

Response 201: `{ success, message, response_count }`

item.description: Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{api_token}}`. Fetch and submit pre/post-event survey answers.

item.name: Notifications

request.description:
NEW — list the student's in-app notifications. The backend already creates these (via admin-triggered announcements, event reminders, etc.) but there was previously NO endpoint to read them — they silently accumulated with no way for the app to ever display them.

Optional query param `unread_only=1` to filter to unread only.

Response 200: `{ success, unread_count, notifications: [{ id, title, body, read_at, created_at }] }`

NEW — mark a single notification as read. Scoped to the authenticated user's own notifications.

Response 200: `{ success: true, message }`. Response 404 if not found / not owned.

item.description: Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{api_token}}`. In-app notifications inbox — entirely new; the backend generated these already but nothing could read them until now.
